title: Circular coredumps by fedora-coreos on aarch64
body:
### Issue Description

Hello, my machine was working fine but at some point, I noticed the vfkit/com.apple.Virtualization.VirtualMachine was eating a lot of CPU on my computer

```
benoitf          68112 362.3  3.5 410577856 3501424   ??  Rs   Wed09AM 452:04.96 /System/Library/Frameworks/Virtualization.framework/Versions/A/XPCServices/com.apple.Virtualization.VirtualMachine.xpc/Contents/MacOS/com.apple.Virtualization.VirtualMachin
```


I logged in to the machine using `podman machine ssh`

And I'm noticing 'system-coredump' processes

![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/436777/0ca095ef-2c63-42ff-94f1-f3586193cc43)


### Steps to reproduce the issue

I don't know how to reproduce, it was happening after some time


### Describe the results you received

Using journalctl I have in the logs

```
Mar 07 10:10:08 localhost.localdomain audit[1416480]: ANOM_ABEND auid=4294967295 uid=980 gid=980 ses=4294967295 subj=system_u:system_r:systemd_coredump_t:s0 pid=1416480 comm="systemd-coredum" exe="/usr/lib/systemd/systemd-coredump" sig=4 res=1
Mar 07 10:10:08 localhost.localdomain systemd-coredump[1416480]: [🡕] Process 1416447 (systemd-coredum) of user 980 dumped core.

                                                                 Module libbz2.so.1 from rpm bzip2-1.0.8-16.fc39.aarch64

                                                                 Module liblzma.so.5 from rpm xz-5.4.4-1.fc39.aarch64

                                                                 Stack trace of thread 1416447:

Mar 07 10:10:08 localhost.localdomain systemd-coredump[1416488]: Removed old coredump core.systemd-coredum.980.213ad329180041c1ba4d4975a4d34462.1303115.1709802042000000.zst.
```

there are tons of these entries

### Describe the results you expected

no dump, system running fine

comments.body:
Sounds more of a problem with some application inside?
Can you run `coredumpctl list` to see what dumps it created? Looks like systemd-coredump itself is crashing.

```

```

It's creating dumps in a loop, I do have 202046 items
```
coredumpctl list | wc -l
202046
```


I just have an empty pod
```
$ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                                  COMMAND     CREATED       STATUS       PORTS                   NAMES
37574be4aaf6  localhost/podman-pause:5.0.0-dev-a66276e1e-1708349020              19 hours ago  Up 19 hours  0.0.0.0:9002->8080/tcp  744424bfee66-infra
```
I've restarted the machine and coredump processes are gone

Yeah so I guess my instinct was right, somehow systemd-coredump kept crashing so it tried to create a crashdump for itself just to crash again... so it looped forever.
SIGILL is interesting because it likely means that the binary uses a cpu instruction not supported by the cpu.

You could try to reproduce by running some custom aarch64 program that just segfaults and see if systemd-coredump start crashing again.
Do you test on a M1, M2 or M3? This likely needs to be reported against the fedora package as it either contains the wrong compiler options for your cpu or it is some upstream bug.

it started after this (from journalctl)
